text: Software testing plays a vital role in creating reliable applications. Developers use different types of tests to ensure their code works correctly. Unit tests check individual pieces of code, like functions or methods, in isolation. They are fast to run and help catch small errors early. In contrast, integration tests verify that different parts of the system work together properly, such as a database connecting to an application. Many teams follow test-driven development, or TDD, where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps guide the design and prevents bugs from appearing later. Together, these practices give developers confidence to make changes and add new features without breaking existing functionality.
